input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:01:49.018Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

Use `SetAppThemeColor` (for colors) or `SetAppTheme<T>` (for any property):

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();

    // Color properties
    this.SetAppThemeColor(BackgroundColorProperty,
        light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
        dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

    myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
        light: Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
        dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

    // Non-color properties (e.g., images)
    myImage.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty,
        light: "sun.png",
        dark:  "moon.png");
}
```

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic** | Both approaches respond to OS theme changes at runtime — no manual subscription required |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define colors in `AppThemeBinding` inside `App.xaml` resources and reference them with `{StaticResource}` |
| **Detect current theme** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified` |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of OS setting |

For larger apps, centralise your colors in `App.xaml` so you update them in one place:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- MainPage.xaml -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```
